raw_output:
```
══════════════════════════════════════════════
  CODE REVIEW REPORT
  Files changed: 1 | Lines added: +85 | Lines removed: -0
══════════════════════════════════════════════

── BLOCKERS (4) ────────────────────────────

[B1] src/api/dashboard.ts:17–25 — N+1 query pattern in getDashboard
     The nested loop fetches a product from the database for every item in every order.
     With 10 orders × 10 items each, this triggers 100 database queries instead of 1–2.
     Fix: Batch-fetch all product IDs using `db.products.findByIds([...productIds])` before
     enrichment, or use a single database join if the ORM supports it.

[B2] src/api/dashboard.ts:62–68 — N+1 query pattern in getRecentlyViewed
     Same issue: one database query per view history item in a loop. With 10 items, that's
     10 queries to fetch the same products sequentially.
     Fix: Batch-fetch using `db.products.findByIds([...productIds])` after querying view history.

[B3] src/api/dashboard.ts:10–31, 40–47, 56–69, 79–85 — Missing error handling
     None of the four functions have try-catch blocks. If any database query fails, the promise
     rejects unhandled. Express will pass it to the error handler, but best practice is to catch
     and send an explicit error response (500 with a message).
     Fix: Wrap each function body in try-catch; send `res.status(500).json({ error: '...' })` on
     database errors.

[B4] src/api/dashboard.ts:24–26 and 65–67 — Null dereference risk
     If a product is deleted but still referenced in order items or view history, accessing
     `product.name`, `product.imageUrl`, `product.price` will crash.
     Fix: Check `if (!product) { /* skip or log */ }` before accessing properties, or ensure
     referential integrity at the database level.

── WARNINGS (1) ────────────────────────────

[W1] src/api/dashboard.ts:42 — Unsafe type assumption
     `orders.length` assumes `orders` is always an array. If `db.orders.find()` returns null or
     undefined, this will fail. Add a guard: `const orders = await db.orders.find(...) || []`.

── SUGGESTIONS (3) ──────────────────────────

[S1] src/api/dashboard.ts:2 — Unused import
     `OrderItem` is imported but never referenced. Remove it.

[S2] src/api/dashboard.ts:56 — Magic number
     The limit of 10 for recently viewed products is hardcoded. Define a constant
     `const RECENT_PRODUCTS_LIMIT = 10;` at the top of the file.

[S3] src/api/dashboard.ts — Response type definitions
     Consider exporting TypeScript types for the response shapes (e.g., `DashboardResponse`,
     `DashboardStatsResponse`) for type safety in API consumers.

── VERDICT ─────────────────────────────────

  ✗ NOT READY — 4 blocker(s) must be resolved before merging.
     - Fix N+1 query patterns with batch fetching.
     - Add comprehensive error handling in all four endpoints.
     - Guard against null product references.
     - Add null check on orders array.

══════════════════════════════════════════════
```

```
── ORCHESTRATOR SUMMARY ────────────────────────
{"blockers": 4, "warnings": 1, "suggestions": 3, "verdict": "NOT_READY"}
────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

blockers.title: src/api/dashboard.ts:17–25 — N+1 query pattern in getDashboard
blockers.body:
The nested loop fetches a product from the database for every item in every order.
With 10 orders × 10 items each, this triggers 100 database queries instead of 1–2.
Fix: Batch-fetch all product IDs using `db.products.findByIds([...productIds])` before
enrichment, or use a single database join if the ORM supports it.

     Fix: Batch-fetch all product IDs using `db.products.findByIds([...productIds])` before
     enrichment, or use a single database join if the ORM supports it.

blockers.title: src/api/dashboard.ts:62–68 — N+1 query pattern in getRecentlyViewed

Same issue: one database query per view history item in a loop. With 10 items, that's
10 queries to fetch the same products sequentially.
Fix: Batch-fetch using `db.products.findByIds([...productIds])` after querying view history.

blockers.title: src/api/dashboard.ts:10–31, 40–47, 56–69, 79–85 — Missing error handling
blockers.body:
None of the four functions have try-catch blocks. If any database query fails, the promise
rejects unhandled. Express will pass it to the error handler, but best practice is to catch
and send an explicit error response (500 with a message).
Fix: Wrap each function body in try-catch; send `res.status(500).json({ error: '...' })` on
database errors.
